mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate on equities and eases financial conditions, so a downward monthly trend in policy rates predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails when rate cuts occur during acute recession stress and equity risk premia widen faster than discount rates fall.
failure_condition: If 20d forward SPX returns are not positive on average after FEDFUNDS declines over rolling 3-month windows, or if the sign flips and the hit rate falls below 50% in easing periods, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in UNRATE above recent levels signals weakening household income and future earnings pressure, so a labor-market deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails when unemployment rises for benign reasons while payroll growth and consumption remain firm.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not produce negative average 20d SPX returns, or if positive returns occur in more than 55% of triggered instances across any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized pickup in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates broadening labor income and retail demand, which supports near-term corporate revenue growth and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism fails if payroll gains do not translate into spending or if sales growth is driven only by price inflation.
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term between PAYEMS and RSAFS has no positive out-of-sample effect on 20d SPX returns, or if joint-strength periods do not outperform single-series periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in INDPRO after a prior weakness marks an industrial recovery that improves earnings expectations for cyclicals and the broader index, implying positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal fails when production improves only because inventories are rebuilt without demand follow-through.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises by more than 1.0% over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if post-threshold INDPRO increases are not followed by positive 60d SPX returns on average, or if the conditional hit rate is below 50% over any 4-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in HOUST indicates stronger construction activity and household formation, which supports cyclical growth expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails when housing starts rise only because of policy distortions while consumer demand stays weak.
failure_condition: Reject if higher HOUST does not correspond to higher subsequent 20d SPX returns, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, real policy rates fall and the market interprets policy as highly accommodative, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next week. The signal fails if inflation acceleration is already fully priced or if it coincides with abrupt risk-off repricing.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above 2.5% while FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show positive 5d SPX returns more often than not, or if the average return is not above the unconditional 5d mean.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE combined with falling PAYEMS identifies a genuine labor-market deterioration that reduces earnings expectations and raises recession odds, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter. The signal fails when unemployment rises due to labor-force re-entry while payrolls remain stable.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor deterioration state does not lead to negative 60d SPX returns on average, or if positive returns exceed 55% in that state over any rolling 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a recessionary regime, improving RSAFS and HOUST indicate demand stabilization before earnings revisions recover, so the combination predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails outside recessions because the same improvements are already priced as routine growth.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST each rise above their 3-month moving averages
evidence_refs: fact:U.S. macro data as of 2014-04 show weak but stabilizing demand indicators
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-positive RSAFS/HOUST regime does not outperform baseline 20d SPX returns in recessionary periods, or if the effect disappears in non-recession periods by construction but still shows strong positive returns there.
